content: We can hypothesize that inserting the multiple-perspective inconsistency sampling from LLM self-reflection mechanisms into the no-supervised-data Self-Refine framework creates a 'multi-view self-feedback loop' that breaks the trap of static, biased self-correction that plagues original Self-Refine. This hybrid framework retains the original advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data, while boosting refinement quality by forcing the LLM to challenge its own initial output from inconsistent solving perspectives instead of just tweaking surface-level flaws.
